summary: Name mimics popular `urlcat` package with hijacked repository metadata
details: Package is published under the @nullzero scope using the name of the popular `urlcat` library. The `repository` field in package.json points at `balazsbotond/urlcat` — the legitimate upstream's GitHub repository — rather than any repository owned by this package's publisher, which is a metadata-hijack pattern intended to lend false credibility to the package on the registry. A `postinstall` script runs `node lib/encoder.js`, but that file only executes `console.log('[trap:prepare] hook executed')` — there is no network I/O, no shell execution, no filesystem writes, no credential reads, and no environment-variable scraping. The main module is a straightforward URL builder. The `[trap:...]` marker in the postinstall output suggests this may be a canary or research package rather than an active attack. Because the package has the shape of a typosquat (name mimicry + repository hijack) but ships no installer-harm payload, it warrants human review to assess intent rather than an automated block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
